task_completion.description: Open the Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml file in a text editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., VS Code, nano, vim) is open showing the contents of either 'Dockerfile' or 'docker-compose.yml' from the /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor directory.

task_completion.description: Modify the infrastructure files to fix the log buffering issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows an environment variable like 'PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1' added to the docker-compose.yml or Dockerfile, or the python command modified to include the '-u' flag (e.g., 'python -u processor.py').

task_completion.description: Modify the infrastructure files to fix the container shutdown hang.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows 'init: true' added to the service in docker-compose.yml, OR 'STOPSIGNAL SIGINT' added to the Dockerfile, OR the CMD instruction in the Dockerfile changed to the exec form (e.g., CMD ["python", "processor.py"]).

task_completion.description: Rebuild and restart the container to apply the infrastructure changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows the execution of 'docker compose up -d --build', 'docker compose up --force-recreate', or similar commands, with output indicating the order-processor container is being recreated or rebuilt.

task_completion.description: Verify that the container stops gracefully in under 3 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows the execution of 'docker compose down' or 'docker stop order-processor'. The command output should ideally show a completion time of less than 3.0s (e.g., 'Stopped ... 0.4s' or similar fast execution indicators), proving the hang is resolved.

integrity.description: Ensure the legacy Python script was not modified to fix the issues.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must not add signal handling (e.g., 'import signal') or manual log flushing (e.g., 'sys.stdout.flush()') to 'processor.py'. All fixes must be visible in the Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with Docker to test the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show real terminal output from the Docker daemon or the Docker Desktop GUI reflecting the container's state, rather than just editing text files without applying them.

integrity.description: Ensure the changes to the infrastructure files were saved.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor should not show an 'unsaved changes' indicator (like a dot on the tab in VS Code) when the agent proceeds to rebuild the container.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory: /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/order-processor. Container name: order-processor. Target shutdown time: < 3.0 seconds (a shutdown taking > 9.0s is considered a timeout/failure). The task strictly requires infrastructure-level fixes (modifying docker-compose.yml or Dockerfile) and forbids modifying the legacy Python script (processor.py).
